data.title: SEC and CFTC Employees Return After Government Shutdown
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, employees of the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) and Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) are set to resume work following a 43-day furlough due to the U.S. government shutdown. The return is scheduled for Thursday, after U.S. President Donald Trump signed a funding bill late Wednesday to restore federal operations. The operational plans of both agencies stipulate that staff should return on the next regularly scheduled workday after the enactment of appropriations legislation, a detail confirmed by acting CFTC chair Caroline Pham in a Thursday post on X.

During the shutdown, both the SEC and CFTC operated with reduced staff and limited functions. The SEC's ability to review applications for exchange-traded funds, including those related to cryptocurrencies, was notably impacted. The CFTC's operations were largely halted, affecting enforcement, market oversight, and regulatory rulemaking efforts. With the government reopening, the agencies are expected to need time to catch up on activities such as reviewing registration applications submitted during the shutdown period. Some companies took advantage of the situation by submitting IPO and ETF applications, anticipating the end of the shutdown.

Jay Dubow, a partner at law firm Troutman Pepper Locke, noted that some companies might have submitted applications to the SEC knowing they would not be reviewed immediately but would at least be in the queue. He also highlighted the risks associated with repeated shutdowns, suggesting that important matters could slip through the cracks. Throughout the shutdown, officials from both agencies continued to speak at conferences, discussing their approach to cryptocurrencies and addressing operational limitations. SEC Chair Paul Atkins remarked on the agency's restricted functions during a conference on October 7, emphasizing the limitations on staff activities.

Before the funding bill was resolved, Atkins mentioned plans for the SEC to consider establishing a token taxonomy anchored in the Howey test, aiming to recognize that investment contracts can conclude. Similarly, Pham indicated that the CFTC had been advocating for the approval of leveraged spot cryptocurrency trading by December. Meanwhile, Michael Selig, chief counsel for the SEC’s crypto task force, is scheduled to appear before the Senate Agriculture Committee as part of President Trump's initiative to confirm him as the next CFTC chair. Although the hearing could have proceeded during the shutdown, Selig's authority would have been limited had he been confirmed. Pham is expected to step down from her acting chair position if Selig is confirmed, but the CFTC would still face leadership challenges, with only one Senate-confirmed commissioner out of the usual five.

data.title: Bitcoin, Ethereum now operate in ‘different monetary’ universes: Data
data.description:
Bitcoin is turning into a savings-focused asset while Ethereum is becoming a high-velocity utility engine, a split that some analysts say is an emerging structural risk.
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/bitcoin-ethereum-now-operate-in-different-monetary-universes-glassnode-keyrock?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: Bitcoin and Ether Show Divergent Trends in 2025
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, a recent report by Glassnode and Keyrock reveals that Bitcoin (BTC) and Ether (ETH) are exhibiting distinct behaviors in the cryptocurrency market. Bitcoin is increasingly resembling a savings-driven asset with low velocity, akin to gold, while Ether is evolving into a productive onchain asset utilized for staking, collateral, and institutional applications.

Bitcoin's dormancy and turnover rates are now more similar to gold than fiat currency, with 61% of Bitcoin remaining unmoved for over a year and a daily turnover rate of just 0.61% of its free float. This positions Bitcoin firmly in the Store-of-Value category, behaving more like a static asset than a currency in motion. In contrast, Ether is demonstrating a different trend, with long-term holders mobilizing coins three times faster than Bitcoin holders, reflecting a utility-driven approach rather than hoarding.

Ether's turnover rate is approximately 1.3% per day, double that of Bitcoin, and a significant portion of Ether is locked in staking or ETFs, contributing to a productive float that supports DeFi and liquid staking systems. Both Bitcoin and Ether are experiencing a decline in exchange balances, with Bitcoin decreasing by 1.5% and Ether by nearly 18%, as coins move into spot ETFs and digital asset investment vehicles. Analysts suggest this migration into institutional custody represents a crucial structural shift, with Bitcoin becoming more like a digital savings bond and Ether serving as the operational backbone of onchain activity.

Despite these divergent trends, some analysts perceive Ethereum's high activity as a potential risk. 10x Research argues that Ethereum's dynamic behavior may indicate structural fragility, especially as Bitcoin continues to dominate institutional treasury flows. A recent report from 10x suggests that shorting Ether could act as a hedge against Bitcoin's increasing institutional momentum. The firm highlights that Ether-focused companies are facing challenges, with diminishing resources affecting the "digital asset treasury" narrative that previously drove accumulation.

BitMine serves as an example, with its treasury structures allowing institutions to acquire Ether at low costs and later sell it to retail investors at a premium, a cycle that appears to be breaking down. Although Ether inflows into company-held treasuries have stagnated in Q4, BitMine has continued to increase its Ether allocation, adding 110,288 ETH on November 10, bringing its total to 3,505,723 ETH. This article does not provide investment advice or recommendations, and readers are encouraged to conduct their own research before making investment decisions.

data.title: Bitcoin On-Chain Metrics Signal a Potential Market Bottom as Bull Score Hits Extreme Lows
data.description:
Bitcoin may be approaching a major cyclical bottom, according to new on-chain data highlighted by Crypto Rover and CryptoQuant. A combination of historically reliable metrics, including Net Unrealized Profit (NUP) and the Bull Score Index, suggests that current conditions closely resemble previous bottom-formation periods, even as price continues to hover around $104,000.
source: https://www.ethnews.com/bitcoin-on-chain-metrics-signal-a-potential-market-bottom-as-bull-score-hits-extreme-lows/

data.title: Solana at a Breaking Point: Fading Memecoin Hype and Alameda Unlocks Test the $140 Support Zone
data.description:
Solana (SOL) is once again under intense market scrutiny as a combination of fading memecoin activity, declining user engagement, and continuous token unlocks by Alameda Research puts pressure on one of crypto’s strongest 2025 performers. Related Reading: Ethereum Ready To Explode To $12,000 By January, Says Tom Lee While institutional inflows via ETFs remain robust, Solana’s ability to defend key technical levels, particularly the $140–$150 demand zone, will determine whether the asset stabilizes or slides into a deeper correction. Memecoin Cooldown Sends User Activity to One-Year Low Solana’s explosive rise in late 2024 and early 2025 was largely fueled by rapid memecoin launches and hyperactive retail speculation. But that frenzy has sharply cooled
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/news/solana/solana-at-a-breaking-point-fading-memecoin-hype-and-alameda-unlocks-test-the-140-support-zone/
